new_classes.label_en: still wine of fresh grapes with PGI, actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol, in containers > 2 L (CN 2204 29 80)
new_classes.label_de: Stillwein aus frischen Weintrauben mit g.g.A., tatsächlicher Alkoholgehalt > 15 % vol, in Behältnissen > 2 L (KN 2204 29 80)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, with an actual alcoholic strength by volume exceeding 15% vol, presented in containers of a capacity exceeding 2 litres.

coverage_explanation: The legal text references CN codes 22042979 and 22042980 together, identifying them as wines with a protected geographical indication (PGI) under Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers > 2 L. The PGI quality, large container constraint, and parent class (StillWineWithPGI2204Large) are all captured. The alcoholic strength threshold (> 15% vol) distinguishing 22042980 from 22042979 (≤ 15% vol) is expressed via a decimalRange restriction. However, the explicit reference to Additional Note 6(a) as the definitional source for PGI cannot be directly expressed as an OWL axiom, and the shared note for both sibling codes (22042979 and 22042980) means the full sibling-pair discriminating criterion is only partially formalised. Overall coverage is good but not complete due to these nuances.
